compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Plan-review PASS (signed, 0 blocking, 10 advisory + 2 overflow). Triage + decisions:
- [P5 f82524 — same-file siblings] FIXED: added WS3 depends_on WS2 so the Pass-2/3/4 extractions (all editing passes.py + workflow_ops.py) SERIALIZE WS1→WS2→WS3→WS4; they are sequential extractions on shared files, not parallelizable.
- [E3 fb843a / F1 fda3b1,f6248c — 'same verdicts' unspecified, effort-framed ACs] The behavioral-equivalence bar for each extraction = the produced plan_review_verdict PARTITION is equal (blocking/advisory/overflow/indeterminate/dropped finding ids + decisions) under a deterministic FakeRunner, pre- vs post-extraction. Each child's offline test asserts exactly that. (Outcome: 'the verdict partition is unchanged', not 'a test asserts…'.)
- [T4 fea58f — risky in-place rewire] The offline verdict-partition-equivalence test IS the parity safeguard for replacing plan-review's internal pass with the kernel call: the rewire is not merged unless the FakeRunner verdict is byte-equal. (Same discipline as the WS1 drift-probe parity gate in the superseded solid-timer-unison work.)
- [E2 ff3eee — live eval gating] The 'small gated live eval' reuses the EXISTING gated-eval CI pattern (the 'container fidelity (live — gated)' job): needs the [eval] extra + model credentials, SKIPS without them, and is OFF the blocking required-checks path. The offline FakeRunner eval is the CI-blocking one.
- [COH fdb3e6 — coach selection ordering] Clarified for WS3: order is (1) gate-on-surviving>0 (0 surviving → no coach call at all); else (2) deterministic applicability filter on each move's triggers/applies_when → the applicable subset; (3) the LLM picks among ONLY the applicable moves (move_id + bounded subject); (4) deterministic template render. The LLM never selects outside the applicable set.
- [E1 f06bc7] 'Pass-1 batching NOT shared' is an explicit NON-GOAL (already reusable via ProductionBatchRunner), not a deliverable — no AC needed. [P6] AC-quality noted. [T7 fd3209] docs are WS4's deliverable (kernel seam + enforcement doc). [G5 overflow] the 4 children + WS1-first ARE the decomposition / thin slice.

compiled_state.description:
# Four-pass review KERNEL: extract the shared evidence → verify → decide → coach framework (plan-review as reference)

## Context

Industry practice and 2025–2026 research converge on a **multi-pass** LLM review: a finder
surfaces cited EVIDENCE against a locked rubric (no model-emitted severity/confidence); a
SEPARATE verifier validates each finding via atomic, independent binary sub-questions; a
DETERMINISTIC policy (not the model) decides severity and blocking; and an affirmative COACH
maps the surviving advisories to a locked move registry. That is **four passes** (the coach is
Pass-4, demonstrated by plan-review) — the framework's name is updated from three-pass to
four-pass.

The framework is no longer hypothetical. The **plan-review gate (epic `5fd2`)** realized the
full four-pass concretely and runs it in production as the *sole* plan-review gate on the v3
workflow engine: Pass-1 finder (evidence contract `{finding, criteria[], evidence[],
scenarios[], impact}`), Pass-2 verifier (severity attributes + a typed binary sub-answer set),
Pass-3 deterministic `pass3_decide`, Pass-4 coach (move registry, rendered deterministically).
Recent work hardened it further (a non-frontier verifier-model default, token-budget verify
chunking, the coach gated on surviving>0). BUT that implementation lives INSIDE
`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` — it is **not a shared kernel**. A second review surface (the
code-review capability, epic `b744`) would otherwise COPY the passes, forking the verification
vocabulary and the deterministic decision math — exactly the silent-drift class this repo
already paid for once (the retired bespoke vs workflow verify divergence).

This epic **extracts** the value-bearing, divergence-dangerous passes into a reusable kernel
that both gates consume, so the decision core and the binary vocabulary cannot fork.

## Supersedes the prior three-pass epic (`9da1`)

This replaces "Realign rebar reviews to three-pass; migrate existing reviewers" because the
scope changed on three counts:
1. It is **four** passes (the coach pass), not three.
2. "Migrate the existing reviewers" is dropped: `src/rebar/llm/code_review.py` is a throwaway
   DEMO, not a migration target — real code review is epic `b744`, which CONSUMES this kernel.
3. The framework is already built (in plan-review), so the work is **extraction + a clean
   consumer seam**, not a from-scratch build. The old epic's 10 overlay-catalog comments are
   inputs to `b744`, not to this epic's deliverable.

## What the kernel is — and is NOT

**SHARED (extract into the kernel — domain-agnostic, divergence-dangerous):**
- **Pass-2** finding-verifier: operates on a findings list + a domain context; emits per finding
  `{severity_attributes, binary}`. Owns the single `verification` output contract (the binary
  sub-question vocabulary + the severity-attribute enums) and the verify orchestration already
  built for plan-review (token-budget chunking, merge-by-global-index, the verifier-model
  default).
- **Pass-3** deterministic decision: the one `pass3_decide` (+ math: validity from the graded
  binary fraction; impact from the ordinal-mapped severity attributes; the conditional
  cited-reference veto; per-criterion thresholds, parameterized).
- **Pass-4** coach MECHANISM: move-selection → deterministic template render → the bounded
  noun-phrase subject validator → the no-free-prose discipline → gate-on-surviving>0; plus a
  pluggable move-registry SCHEMA.

**NOT shared (already reusable / engine-provided):**
- Pass-1 criteria→batch orchestration — the `ProductionBatchRunner` already does token/budget
  finder batching; the workflow `batch` step already aggregates. Not re-abstracted.
- The workflow shell (steps/branch/batch/`RunnerAgentStep`/`gate_dispatch`) — the v3 engine
  already provides it; each gate keeps a thin workflow.

**Per-gate PLUG-INS (the consumer seam):** criteria + routing, finder prompts, the
domain-context assembler (plan text vs diff), the verify-prompt preamble, and the move-catalog
CONTENT (planning moves vs code moves).

## Enforcement — keep reuse honest WITHOUT making the workflow system brittle

Enforce STRUCTURE mechanically and BEHAVIOR via evals; never enforce prompt *text* via a lint
(an anti-pattern — DSPy / Guardrails / Instructor / promptfoo all enforce typed contracts +
output/behavior, not prompt-string greps):
- **Vocabulary** → ONE registered `verification` schema. Validate via the existing TOLERANT
  stack (json-repair + bounded retry + pydantic-ai native outputs) and DEGRADE to INDETERMINATE
  on an unparseable turn — never hard-crash a gate. Keep the schema small/flat (the
  reliability cost of strict validation is the failure mode to avoid).
- **Decision interpretation** → ONE `pass3_decide` function (thresholds parameterized per
  criterion).
- **Soft prompt rules** (atomicity; independence — don't show the finding's own conclusion;
  allow "insufficient"; verdict-with-citation-NOT-fix) → a canonical RULES SCAFFOLD + kernel
  doc for discoverability, ENFORCED by BEHAVIORAL evals (deterministic FakeRunner assertions on
  the gate path in CI + a small GATED live eval kept OFF the blocking CI path). **No
  prompt-marker lint** (explicitly rejected as brittle/gameable).
- **Move registry** → registered schema + load-time validation, with a `triggers`/applies_when
  applicability field.
- **Drift** → reuse the existing contract / derived-prompt-index drift gate (it keys on the
  output CONTRACT, not on prompt wording).

## Out of scope (decisions recorded, not deliverables)

- **Completion verifier stays single-pass.** It is already binary-per-given-criterion; a full
  independent verify pass ≈2× the agentic cost for a small, unmeasured accuracy gain. The one
  cheap lever (the binary/independence discipline in-prompt) was applied separately, not here.
- The throwaway `code_review.py` demo reviewers (real code review = `b744`).
- Threshold/severity calibration (separately deferred; thresholds start high → mostly advisory).

## Success Criteria

- A shared four-pass kernel module exists (Pass-2 verifier + `verification` contract; Pass-3
  `pass3_decide`; the Pass-4 coach mechanism + a pluggable move-registry schema).
- The plan-review gate is rewired to CONSUME the kernel for Pass-2/3/4 with NO behavior change
  (same verdicts), proving the extraction is reuse rather than a fork.
- A documented consumer SEAM (the per-gate plug-points + the rules scaffold) that `b744` builds
  on without copying the passes.
- Enforcement is structural (schemas) + behavioral (evals), with no prompt-text lint;
  structured-output validation degrades, never hard-crashes a gate.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A shared kernel module (e.g. `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/`) houses: Pass-2
      `verify_findings(findings, *, context, model, headroom)`, the registered `verification`
      contract, Pass-3 `pass3_decide` (+ math, per-criterion thresholds parameterized), and the
      Pass-4 coach mechanism + move-registry schema.
- [ ] The plan-review gate consumes the kernel for Pass-2/3/4; an offline (FakeRunner) test
      asserts the produced verdict is unchanged vs the pre-extraction behavior (reuse, not fork).
- [ ] The `verification` contract is the single source of the binary-question vocabulary +
      severity-attribute enums; validation uses json-repair + bounded retry and DEGRADES to
      INDETERMINATE (never crashes a gate) on an unparseable turn.
- [ ] The move-registry schema includes a `triggers`/applies_when field; coach move-selection
      applies a DETERMINISTIC applicability filter, then the LLM picks among the applicable moves
      (closing the prior wholly-LLM-delegated selection gap).
- [ ] A canonical verifier-rules scaffold + kernel doc record the soft prompt rules; a
      BEHAVIORAL eval (offline FakeRunner on the gate path; a small gated live eval off the
      blocking CI path) verifies the rules are obeyed. NO prompt-marker lint is added.
- [ ] A documented consumer seam enumerates the per-gate plug-points (criteria, finder prompts,
      domain-context assembler, verify preamble, move-catalog) that `b744` consumes; this epic
      `blocks` `b744`.
- [ ] Out-of-scope decisions are recorded (completion verifier single-pass + rationale;
      throwaway code_review.py not migrated; calibration deferred).
- [ ] `make lint` + `make check` + the full test suite pass.
compiled_state.title: Four-pass review kernel: extract the shared evidence->verify->decide->coach framework (plan-review as reference)
